summary: Re-tested both iteration_5 action items. (1) scheduler._run_analysis_job now fully delegates to alerts.send_alert_if_allowed — verified by static check (no `send_strong_dip_alert` reference in scheduler.py) AND functional test invoking the private async function with mocked + real alerts gate. NO NameError, sent tickers correctly populate _status['last_alerts'], rule_blocked/deduped tickers correctly excluded. (2) /api/alerts/today now sorts by 'last_alerted_at' (the field alerts.send_alert_if_allowed writes). Added 6 new scheduler tests (test_scheduler.py). Full regression: 53 passed, 2 skipped (live-data dependent), 0 failed.
critical_code_review_comments: Verified: scheduler.py is now clean. Imports `from alerts import send_alert_if_allowed`, and _run_analysis_job loops `outcome = await send_alert_if_allowed(db, r); if outcome['status']=='sent': alerts_sent.append(...)`. The legacy inline dedupe block + undefined send_strong_dip_alert call is GONE.
Verified: server.py /api/alerts/today now uses `.sort("last_alerted_at", -1)` — matches the field written by alerts.send_alert_if_allowed.

Carry-forward (non-blocking, from iteration_5): /api/recommended-action, /api/discover, /api/investment-plan each independently scan MARKET_UNIVERSE on every request — the Dashboard fires all three in parallel on mount, executing the same 40-ticker scan 3x. Consider a short-TTL cached helper scan_universe(). Not a regression; not in this iteration's scope.
Carry-forward (non-blocking): alerts.passes_alert_rules score comparison is not type-coerced — defensive isinstance(score, (int,float)) guard would harden against future scorer changes.

success_rate.backend: 100% (53/53 pytest; 2 conditional skips for live-data-dependent branches: 'no opportunities today' fallback and 'demote strong to accumulate' branch — both require live RSI states that don't currently occur on the watchlist)

seed_data_creation: test_run_analysis_job_routes_through_send_alert_if_allowed uses an in-memory mock of send_alert_if_allowed (no DB writes). test_run_analysis_job_with_real_send_alert_if_allowed_does_not_nameerror passes only rule_blocked rows so alerts gate exits before any DB access. test_alerts.py continues to populate alert_log via /api/trigger-alerts (currently rule_blocks all live watchlist tickers, so alert_log stays clean).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_5 critical bug (scheduler NameError) and minor bug (/api/alerts/today sort key) are both FIXED and verified. New regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_scheduler.py — keep it. Strict alert rule (score>=70 AND RSI<=40 AND signal=='Strong') is enforced consistently in: alerts.passes_alert_rules, alerts.send_alert_if_allowed, /api/trigger-alerts, /api/recommended-action (Buy Now), AND scheduler._run_analysis_job (via delegation). /api/alerts/today writes match reads (last_alerted_at). Mongo unique compound index on alert_log (ticker,date) confirmed. Live watchlist (5 stocks) currently rule_blocks all tickers so /api/trigger-alerts.sent is empty — this is expected, not a bug. To run tests locally: `export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://value-signal-1.preview.emergentagent.com && pytest backend/tests/ -v`.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration_5 RCA was correct: scheduler.py had a leftover inline dedupe block calling undefined send_strong_dip_alert. E1 fixed it by replacing the entire block (lines 49-70 in old file) with the 4-line delegating loop. Confirmed by reading current scheduler.py (lines 49-56) and by static scan that ensures the symbol 'send_strong_dip_alert' no longer appears anywhere in scheduler.py. Functional test invoking _run_analysis_job with both mocked and real alerts gate passes — no NameError under any path.
